meta.title: Mundiales de Fútbol
meta.description: The definitive encyclopedia of the FIFA Men's World Cup (1930–2026): results, squads, minute-by-minute events, referees, stadiums and coverage in five languages.

hero.subtitle: The definitive encyclopedia of the FIFA World Cup, down to the last pass.
hero.videoFallbackAlt: A football stadium seen from above

countdown.label: In
countdown.liveNow: The World Cup has kicked off

groups.title: The 12 groups of the World Cup

encyclopedia.title: Encyclopedia
encyclopedia.text: Squads, referees, stadiums, attendance and events for every World Cup match in history.

editorial.title: Storytelling
editorial.text: Long reads, videos and reports that tell the why behind the numbers.

interactive.title: Interactive
interactive.text: Player comparators, simulators, quizzes and a prediction game for 2026.

footer.tagline: A living library of the FIFA World Cup.
footer.credits: Built on open data from FIFA, Wikipedia, StatsBomb and contributors. Imagery via Wikimedia Commons.

contact.title: Write to us
contact.intro: A correction, new data, a collaboration proposal, or want to exercise your data-protection rights? This form lands straight in our inbox.

form.consent: I have read and accept the
form.successBody: Your message arrived. We will reply in the coming days.
form.errorRateLimit: Too many submissions from your IP. Try again later.
form.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Try again in a few minutes.

wc2026Bar.label: World Cup 2026
wc2026Bar.postSubtitle: Revisit the road to the title

cookies.title: This site uses cookies
cookies.intro: We use strictly necessary cookies for the site to work and, if you agree, analytics cookies to measure usage. None are shared with advertisers.

necessary.title: Necessary
necessary.desc: Essential for the site to work. Cannot be turned off.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.desc: Help us understand which pages matter. Anonymous and aggregated.

marketing.title: Marketing
marketing.desc: We don't use any marketing cookies today.

wc2026.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 · Full schedule, venues, groups and kick-off times
wc2026.description: FIFA World Cup 2026 (USA, Mexico, Canada): complete schedule for all 104 matches, 16 stadiums and 12 groups. When it starts and when the final is played.

dondeVer.title: Where to watch the 2026 World Cup in Spain · DAZN, Movistar Plus+ and free options
dondeVer.description: How to watch every 2026 FIFA World Cup match in Spain: Movistar Plus+ via DAZN, free-to-air options, Spain national-team fixtures, schedule and prices updated April 2026.
dondeVer.intro: How to watch all 104 matches of the 2026 World Cup in Spain: Movistar Plus+, DAZN, free options and tips to watch Spain vs Cape Verde and Uruguay without missing a minute. Information updated April 2026.
dondeVer.abroadTitle: Watching the World Cup from outside Spain

entradas.title: 2026 World Cup Tickets · How to buy, prices and sales phases
entradas.description: Complete guide to buying 2026 FIFA World Cup tickets: official prices by category, Phase 4 sales open until the final, hospitality packages and direct links to FIFA Tickets.

panini.title: Panini 2026 World Cup · Sticker album, packs and prices
panini.description: Everything about the official Panini 2026 World Cup sticker album: expected release date, price, most sought-after stickers, special editions, where to buy in Spain and trading communities.
panini.intro: The official sticker collection of the first 48-team World Cup. Release date, prices, special editions, most wanted stickers and where to find them in Spain. Updated April 2026.

grupoH.title: Spain in Group H of the 2026 World Cup · Cape Verde and Uruguay
grupoH.description: Spain in Group H of the 2026 World Cup: opponents (Cape Verde, Uruguay), schedule, venues (Atlanta and Guadalajara), forecast, key players and where to watch.
grupoH.intro: Three matches, two host countries (USA and Mexico) and the first step toward a possible repeat of the 2010 title. Schedule, opponents, venues, players and where to watch.

historias.title: Stories and anecdotes from the FIFA World Cups
historias.description: The quotes, controversies, prophecies and narratives that have shaped the history of the World Cups: Hand of God, Maracanazo, Iniesta's goal, Lineker on the Germans… Anecdote by anecdote, all the way to the 2026 World Cup.

galeria.title: Photo gallery · National teams across the World Cups
galeria.description: A curated gallery of the most iconic World Cup images. Champion squads, celebrations, stadiums and trophies from 1930 to 2026. Every photo released under a free licence.
galeria.intro: The images that have told the story of the national teams across a century of World Cup football. {n} curated photos, all under free licence, credited to their original authors.

edicionesList.title: Editions · 1930-2026
edicionesList.description: All 23 editions of the men's FIFA World Cup, from Uruguay 1930 to the 2026 tournament in the United States, Mexico and Canada. Results, hosts, champions and stories.

jugadoresList.title: Players · Top World Cup performers and all-time top scorers
jugadoresList.description: The players with the most World Cup appearances, the all-time top scorers and the legends who changed football. Pelé, Maradona, Messi, Klose, Ronaldo and the rest.

seleccionesList.title: National teams · Every World Cup nation
seleccionesList.description: The national teams that have competed in a FIFA World Cup: champions, finalists and debutants. From Brazil and Germany to the sides that have only touched the trophy once.

estadiosList.title: Stadiums · Historic World Cup venues
estadiosList.description: Every stadium that has hosted World Cup matches, from Estadio Centenario in Montevideo to the Azteca, including Wembley, Maracanã and Lusail.

fanZone.title: Fan zone 2026 World Cup · How to set up your home for the 64 matches
fanZone.description: How to gear up your home for the 2026 World Cup: 4K TVs, soundbars, projectors and rituals. The screen, the sound and the people that turn the tournament into something more than an event.

sedes.intro: From the Estadio Azteca in Mexico City to MetLife Stadium in New York/New Jersey, plus Vancouver, Toronto, Atlanta, Dallas and ten more host cities. Each venue has its stadium, schedule, travel plan, hotel districts and on-the-ground tips.
sedes.mapHint: Tap a marker to see the stadium, matches and travel guide. Green MEX, blue USA, red CAN.
sedes.sedesCount: {count, plural, =1 {1 host city} other {# host cities}}
sedes.title: World Cup 2026 host cities: all 16 venues, matches and travel guide
sedes.description: All 16 host cities of the 2026 FIFA World Cup across the USA, Mexico and Canada. Schedule by city, stadium, capacity, what to do, where to stay and how to get there.

calendario.title: 2026 World Cup schedule: 104 matches by stage, dates and kick-off times
calendario.description: Full 2026 World Cup schedule broken down by stage: groups, round of 32, round of 16, quarter-finals, semi-finals, third-place match and final. Dates, kick-off times and venues.
calendario.intro: Structure of the 2026 World Cup: the first edition with 48 teams introduces a new round of 32 between the group stage and the classic round of 16. The tournament runs for 39 days, from the opening match at the Estadio Azteca to the final at MetLife Stadium.
calendario.phaseGroupDesc: 72 matches across 12 groups (round-robin of 6 matches per group). The top 2 plus the 8 best third-placed sides go through, 32 teams in total.
calendario.phaseR32Desc: 16 single-leg knockouts never seen before at a World Cup: the first cut-off, the winner advances to the round of 16. A novelty of the 48-team format.
calendario.phaseR16Desc: 8 single-leg knockouts. By now the tournament's heavyweights are in. Winners head to the quarter-finals.
calendario.phaseQFDesc: 4 knockout ties. After a rest day, the tournament's final sprint kicks off.
calendario.phaseSFDesc: 2 matches at two different venues. The winners play the final, the losers contest the third-place match.
calendario.phase3PDesc: The consolation match for the eliminated semi-finalists.
calendario.phaseFinalDesc: 19 July at MetLife Stadium (New York/New Jersey). The champions lift the trophy.
calendario.fixturesPending: Match-ups will be confirmed once the previous stage ends.

grupos.title: 2026 World Cup groups: A-L with the 48 teams, host cities and schedule
grupos.description: The 12 groups of the 2026 World Cup (A-L) with the 48 teams, flags, host cities for each match and full group-stage schedule.
grupos.intro: Final draw held on 5 December 2025 at the Kennedy Center in Washington D.C. The top 2 from each group plus the 8 best third-placed sides go through, 32 teams in total to a brand-new round of 32 never seen before at a World Cup.
grupos.qualifyKicker: How teams qualify for the round of 32
grupos.qualifyBody: Each group plays 6 matches in a round-robin (everyone vs everyone). The top 2 from each group go through (24 teams) plus the 8 best third-placed sides across the tournament (out of 12 candidates). Total: 32 teams to a round of 32 played from 28 June to 3 July.

wc2026Hub.intro: 48 nations. 104 matches. 16 cities. 3 countries. The first World Cup of the new format.
wc2026Hub.groupsIntro: The top 2 plus the 8 best third-placed sides advance to a brand-new round of 32, never seen before at a World Cup. Mexico open the tournament in Group A, Canada play in B and the United States in D.

dondeVerExtra.abroadTitle: Watching the World Cup from outside Spain

entradasExtra.intro: How to buy, what prices to expect and which sales phases are still open for the 104 matches of the first 48-team World Cup. Guide updated to April 2026.
entradasExtra.warningTitle: FIFA is the only official seller.
entradasExtra.warningText: Any website offering tickets at face value outside fifa.com/tickets is unauthorised resale or a scam. Your ticket can be cancelled at the stadium gate.
entradasExtra.phasesTitle: When they were sold and what is still left
entradasExtra.footerHeading: Buy them on the official website
entradasExtra.footerText: This page is informational: we do not sell or resell tickets. For a safe transaction always use fifa.com/tickets or the official FIFA resale platform.

fanZoneExtra.intro: How to gear up your home for the 104 matches of the 2026 World Cup: the screen, the sound, the food, the rituals that turn the tournament into something more than an event.

paniniExtra.intro: The official sticker album for the first 48-team World Cup. Release date, prices, special editions, the most sought-after stickers and trades.

records.topScorersIntro: The all-time scoring ranking at FIFA World Cups. Klose leads with 16, Ronaldo is second with 15, Müller has 14. Messi and Mbappé are the active players who have climbed the most.
records.yellowsIntro: The flip side of fair play. Javier Mascherano leads the all-time list with seven yellows, several of them in finals and semi-finals. A yellow at a World Cup is always a decision: take a foul to avoid losing your opponent.
records.yellowsDisclaimer: Data extracted from matches with minute-by-minute events recorded in our database. Coverage is complete from the 1994 World Cup; earlier tournaments may have unrecorded cards if the official report was sparse.
records.redsIntro: A red card at a World Cup is almost always the end of a tournament. Only a handful of players have been sent off in different editions, with Rigobert Song the most well-known case. Here are all of them.
records.redsDisclaimer: Full coverage from the 1994 World Cup. Before that edition, sendings-off are recorded only when the official report is available: players such as Rigobert Song (1994, 1998) and Zinedine Zidane (1998, 2006), with two reds each, are the known historic ceiling.
records.minutesIntro: Beyond the squad lists, the actual minutes on the pitch. Paolo Maldini held the all-time record for two decades with 2,217 minutes in 23 matches (1990-2002), until Lionel Messi surpassed him at Qatar 2022. Lothar Matthäus, in turn, still holds the matches-played record with 25 (1982-1998), now also matched by Messi with 26.
records.minutesDisclaimer: Absolute record: Lionel Messi (Argentina) overtook Paolo Maldini's 2,217 minutes in the Qatar 2022 final. Our minute-by-minute coverage is complete from 1994; earlier tournaments may underestimate minutes when the official report did not detail substitution times.
records.wcsPlayedIntro: Only six players in history have played in five World Cups: Antonio Carbajal (Mexico, 1950-1966), Lothar Matthäus (Germany, 1982-1998), Rafael Márquez (Mexico, 2002-2018), Andrés Guardado (Mexico, 2006-2022), Lionel Messi (Argentina, 2006-2022) and Cristiano Ronaldo (Portugal, 2006-2022). Messi and CR7 could add a sixth in 2026.
records.wcsPlayedDisclaimer: Absolute record: 6 players with 5 World Cups (list above). In matches the ranking is led by Messi with 26, followed by Matthäus with 25 and CR7 with 22.
records.ownGoalsIntro: The most expensive own goal in World Cup history was scored by Andrés Escobar against the United States in 1994 (Colombia knocked out in the group stage). Ten days later, Escobar was murdered in Medellín. Here are all the official own goals at the tournament since 1930.
records.ownGoalsDisclaimer: Trivia: no player has scored more than one own goal in World Cup history. There are players who scored at both ends in the same tournament (Ernie Brandts in 1978, Ruud Krol in 1974, Marcelo in 2018). Russia 2018 broke the single-edition record with 12 own goals; the cumulative total from 1930 to 2022 exceeds 50.
records.teamCardsIntro: Brazil leads the all-time sendings-off record (10 reds across 22 World Cups played). Argentina (9) and Uruguay (8) complete the South American podium. Every match counts: the total is cumulative, not per edition.
records.teamCardsDisclaimer: The ranking reflects the cumulative total, not relative discipline. Brazil, Argentina and Uruguay sit at the top in part because they have the most appearances (22, 18 and 14 World Cups respectively). Minute-by-minute card coverage is complete from 1994; in earlier tournaments the records may underestimate light bookings not captured in the official report.

mascotas.title: 2026 World Cup mascots: Maple, Zayu and Clutch · Canada, Mexico and USA
mascotas.description: The three official mascots of the 2026 FIFA World Cup represent the host countries: Maple (moose, Canada), Zayu (jaguar, Mexico) and Clutch (bald eagle, USA). History, symbolism and FIFA design.
mascotas.intro: For the first time in history, the World Cup has three official mascots — one per host country. A moose, a jaguar and a bald eagle represent Canada, Mexico and the United States in the first 48-team tournament. FIFA design unveiled in September 2025.
